commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content:
And there are some, again, who relegate the death of Adam to the thousandth year; for since "a day of the Lord is as a thousand years".
This is an account of the things formerly created, as also it is a prophecy of what is to come. For the day of the Lord is as a thousand years;

commentaries.author: Justin Martyr
commentaries.content: For as Adam was told that in the day he ate of the tree he would die, we know that he did not complete a thousand years. We have perceived, moreover, that the expression, `The day of the Lord is as a thousand years, '

commentaries.author: Lucius Caecilius Firmianus Lactantius
commentaries.content: For the great day of God is limited by a circle of a thousand years, as the prophet shows, who says
